sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · RISK MANAGEMENT
sheet.title: Chapter 10 – Risk Assessment & Mitigation
sheet.p: Every software solution introduces technical and operational risks that should be identified during the design phase. Evaluating these risks early enables the team to define appropriate mitigation strategies and reduces the likelihood of unexpected issues during deployment or production.
The following table summarizes the primary risks identified for the Theta Chat Bot project.

row.cell: Implement event-driven incremental synchronization whenever content is published or updated.
Use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and restrict responses to retrieved website content.
Display a user-friendly fallback message and monitor provider availability. Future support for multiple LLM providers can improve resilience.
Monitor token usage, analyze operational costs, and optimize prompts and retrieval strategies.
Apply confidence thresholds and direct users to the Contact or Inquiry page when sufficient information is unavailable.
Scale the FastAPI backend horizontally and optimize database performance.
Maintain regular database backups and validate restoration procedures.
Implement structured logging, health monitoring, and automated alerting.

sheet.p: Several technical risks are associated with AI-powered chatbot systems.

jfield.val: Retrieved website content does not closely match the user's question.
Semantic embeddings, optimized chunking strategy, similarity threshold tuning, and continuous evaluation.
Outdated website content may result in inaccurate responses.
Event-driven incremental synchronization, metadata versioning, and history tracking.
Reliance on a single LLM provider may introduce operational risks.
Provider-independent AI abstraction and support for future LLM integration.

sheet.p: Operational risks relate to deployment, infrastructure, and ongoing maintenance.

jfield.val: Server downtime, database issues, Docker failures, and misconfigured environments.
Health monitoring, container restart policies, backups, and deployment validation.

sheet.p: Business-related risks should also be considered.

jfield.val: Out-of-scope questions, outdated content, and increased operational costs.
Define scope clearly, maintain website as source of truth, and monitor API costs.

sheet.p: Risks should be reviewed periodically during development and after production deployment.
The review process involves the following sequential checks:

steps.step: Metrics Analysis: Review API costs, token usage, and latency.
Feedback Review: Analyze thumbs up/down and explicit user feedback.
Query Audit: Identify failed, unanswered, or low-confidence queries.
Quality Check: Assess the accuracy and hallucination rate of AI responses.
Knowledge Audit: Expand knowledge base coverage to address gaps.

sheet.p: Continuous review allows the chatbot to evolve while maintaining reliability and accuracy.

row.cell: Minimizes hallucinations by grounding responses in approved content.
Ensures knowledge remains current without unnecessary processing.
Enables early detection of technical issues.
Reduces dependency on a single AI provider and improves future flexibility.
